# clictl Registry

> Official tool registry for clictl - the package manager for your Agent. Curated specs for APIs, CLIs, websites, MCP servers, and skills.

This registry contains YAML spec files that describe tools. Agents use these specs to call APIs, run CLI commands, query websites, connect to MCP servers, and install skills.

## How to create a spec

Read SPEC_SCHEMA.md for the complete schema reference with templates and examples.

## File structure

- `specs/{first-letter}/<tool-name>/<tool-name>.yaml` - All tool specs (APIs, CLIs, websites, MCP servers, skills)
- `SPEC_SCHEMA.md` - Complete spec schema reference (read this first)
- `README.md` - Overview and contributing guide

## Key rules

- Every API/CLI action must have `assert` (at least `status: [200]`) and `transform`
- POST actions that are read-only must include `safe: true`
- Auth is an array: `auth: [{ type: api_key, key_env: FOO, inject: { location: header, param: Authorization, prefix: "Bearer" } }]`
- Destructive actions (DELETE, state-changing CLI commands) must include `destructive` in tags
- Destructive actions are controlled by workspace ACL security rules
- No em dashes in any content

## Valid categories

ai, cloud, communication, crypto, data, data-portal, developer, devops, documentation, finance, geo, ip, knowledge-base, media, monitoring, news, productivity, reference, search, security, text, weather

## Spec format summary

```yaml
name: tool-name
description: One line description
version: "1.0"
category: developer
tags: [tag1, tag2]
protocol: http
owner_name: Company
website: https://example.com
support_url: https://docs.example.com
pricing_model: freemium
connection:
  base_url: https://api.example.com
  timeout: 15s
  headers:
    Accept: application/json
auth:
  - type: api_key
    key_env: EXAMPLE_KEY
    inject:
      location: header
      param: Authorization
      prefix: "Bearer"
actions:
  - name: get-data
    description: Fetch data
    method: GET
    path: /data
    params:
      - name: q
        type: string
        required: true
        in: query
    assert:
      - status: [200]
    transform:
      - extract: "$.data"
      - truncate: { max_items: 20 }
```

## MCP spec format summary

MCP specs use `protocol: mcp` and define a `transport` block instead of `connection`, and a `tools` block instead of `actions`.

```yaml
name: my-mcp-server
description: One line description
version: "1.0"
category: developer
tags: [mcp, tag1, tag2]
protocol: mcp
owner_name: Author Name
website: https://example.com
support_url: https://github.com/org/repo/issues
pricing_model: free

transport:
  type: stdio                     # stdio or http
  command: npx
  args: ["-y", "@scope/mcp-server"]
  requires:
    - name: node
      url: https://nodejs.org
      check: "node --version"

auth:
  - type: none

tools:
  expose:                          # all, or list of tools
    - name: tool_name
      description: "What the tool does"
    - name: dangerous_tool
      destructive: true            # Requires ACL approval
  deny:                            # Block specific tools
    - name: blocked_tool
  tool_transforms:                 # Output transforms per tool
    tool_name:
      - truncate: { max_length: 8000 }

prompts:
  system: |
    Instructions for the agent when using this server.
  tool_instructions:
    tool_name: "Additional context for this tool."

resources:
  expose: all                      # all, or list of URI patterns

install_mode:
  default: both                    # skill | mcp | both
  allow_mcp: true
  allow_skill: true
```

## MCP key rules

- MCP specs must have `protocol: mcp` and a `transport` block
- MCP specs must have a `tools` block (expose: all, or a list of tool names)
- Tools marked `destructive: true` are controlled by workspace ACL rules
- Tools in the `deny` list are never exposed regardless of ACL
- `prompts.system` is injected when the server is active
- `prompts.tool_instructions` are appended to individual tool descriptions
- `install_mode` controls whether the server is installed as a clictl skill (proxied), native MCP, or both

## Skill spec format summary

Skill specs use `protocol: skill` and define a `source` block (where to get the SKILL.md) and `platforms` block (which platforms support it). No `connection` or `actions` needed.

```yaml
name: my-skill
description: What this skill teaches the agent to do
version: "1.0"
category: developer
tags: [skill, tag1, tag2]
protocol: skill
owner_name: Author Name
website: https://github.com/org/repo
support_url: https://github.com/org/repo/issues
pricing_model: free
license: Apache-2.0

source:
  type: github                     # github, npm, or inline
  repo: org/skills-repo
  path: skills/my-skill/SKILL.md
  ref: main

platforms:
  - name: claude-code
    install_method: skill
    config_path: .claude/skills/
  - name: cursor
    install_method: skill
    config_path: .cursor/skills/
  - name: vscode
    install_method: copy
    config_path: .github/skills/

skill_metadata:
  author: org-name
  version: "1.0"

auth:
  - type: none

requires_tools: [bash, read, write]    # Agent tools the skill expects
requires_mcp: [filesystem-server]      # MCP servers the skill needs (optional)
```

## Skill key rules

- Skill specs must have `protocol: skill` and a `source` block
- Skill specs must have a `platforms` block listing supported platforms
- `source.type` is one of: `github`, `npm`, `inline`
- For `github` source: `repo` (org/repo) and `path` (path to SKILL.md) are required
- `requires_tools` lists agent tools the skill expects to be available
- `requires_mcp` lists MCP servers the skill depends on (resolved during install)
- Skills don't use `connection` or `actions` blocks (they are instruction-based, not function-based)

## Validation

```bash
uv run pytest tests/test_schema_all.py -k "<tool-name>" -v
```
